import re

# normalization function
arabic_diacritics = re.compile("""
                             [\u0610-\u061A\u064B-\u065F\u06D6-\u06ED]  # diacritics and other marks
                             """, re.VERBOSE)
tatweel = '\u0640'  # tatweel char
def normalize_arabic(text):
    if not isinstance(text, str):
        return ''
    text = text.strip()
    # remove diacritics
    text = arabic_diacritics.sub('', text)
    # remove tatweel
    text = text.replace(tatweel, '')
    # unify alef variants to bare alef
    text = re.sub('[أإآا]', 'ا', text)
    # normalize hamza on waw/yeh maybe: no change for ؤ ئ for now, but unify them to و and ي?? Danger. We'll keep them.
    # remove punctuation and non-letter/digit characters but keep Arabic letters and spaces
    # Replace punctuation with space
    text = re.sub(r'[^\w\u0621-\u064A]+', ' ', text)  # keep Arabic letters and latin digits/underscore (if any)
    # collapse spaces
    text = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', text)
    text = text.strip()
    # lowercase not needed
    return text

# test
print(normalize_arabic("لا يَرْجُو عَبْدٌ إِلَّا اللَّهَ"))

query_norm = normalize_arabic("لا يَرْجُو عَبْدٌ إِلَّا اللَّهَ")

for i, entry in enumerate(hadith_data):

with open(quran_path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    quran_data = json.load(f)
len(quran_data)

from collections import Counter
query = "لا يَرْجُو عَبْدٌ إِلَّا اللَّهَ"
qnorm = normalize_arabic(query)
qtokens = qnorm.split()
def token_f1(a_tokens, b_tokens):
    a_counts = Counter(a_tokens)
    b_counts = Counter(b_tokens)
    common = sum(min(a_counts[t], b_counts[t]) for t in a_counts.keys() & b_counts.keys())
    if common==0:
        return 0.0
    prec = common / sum(b_counts.values())
    rec = common / sum(a_counts.values())
    return 2*prec*rec/(prec+rec)

# compute top matches by token f1
scores=[]
for i, entry in enumerate(hadith_data):
    txt = entry.get('hadithTxt','') or entry.get('Matn','')
    norm = normalize_arabic(txt)
    tokens = norm.split()
    f1 = token_f1(qtokens, tokens)
    if f1>0:
        scores.append((f1, i, norm[:200], txt[:200]))
scores_sorted = sorted(scores, reverse=True)[:30]
scores_sorted[:20], len(scores_sorted)

import re

patterns = [
    r'\bلا\b.*\bيرجو\b.*\bعبد\b.*\bالا\b.*\bالله\b',
    r'\bلا\b.*\bعبد\b.*\bيرجو\b.*\bالا\b.*\bالله\b',
    r'\bعبد\b.*\bيرجو\b.*\bالا\b.*\bالله\b',
    r'\bيرجو\b.*\bعبد\b.*\bالا\b.*\bالله\b',
    r'\bلا\b.*\bيرجو\b.*\bالا\b.*\bالله\b',
    r'\bلا\b.*\bيرجو\b.*\bالله\b',
    r'\bيرجو\b.*\bالله\b',
    r'\bلا\b.*\bيرجو\b'
]
matches = []
for i, entry in enumerate(hadith_data):
    txt = entry.get('hadithTxt','') or entry.get('Matn','')
    norm = normalize_arabic(txt)
    for pat in patterns:
        if re.search(pat, norm):
            matches.append((i, entry, norm))
            break
len(matches)

for idx, entry, norm in matches:
    print("INDEX", idx)
    print("norm:", norm)
    print("original excerpt:", (entry.get('hadithTxt','') or entry.get('Matn',''))[:400])
    print("-"*60)

# search for entries containing 'لا يرجو' or 'لا يرجون' or 'لا يرجون' variants
variants = ['لا يرجو', 'لا يرجون', 'لا يرجى', 'لا يرجون', 'لا يرجى', 'لا يرجوا']
res_var = []
for i, entry in enumerate(hadith_data):
    txt = entry.get('hadithTxt','') or entry.get('Matn','')
    norm = normalize_arabic(txt)
    for v in variants:
        if normalize_arabic(v) in norm:
            res_var.append((i, entry, norm))
            break
len(res_var)

for idx, entry, norm in res_var:
    print(idx)
    print(norm)
    print(entry.get('hadithTxt','')[:400])
    print('-'*80)

variants = [
    "لا يرجو العبد الا الله",
    "لا يرجو عبد الا الله",
    "لا يرجو الا الله",
    "لا يَرجو العبد الا الله",
    "لا يَرجو عبد الا الله",
    "لا يَرجو الا الله",
    "لا يرجو عبدٌ الا الله",

    "لا يرجو العبد إلا الله",
    "لا يَرجُو العبد إلا الله",
    "لا يَرجُو عبد إلا الله"
]
found=[]
for v in variants:
    vnorm = normalize_arabic(v)
    for i, entry in enumerate(hadith_data):
        txt = entry.get('hadithTxt','') or entry.get('Matn','')
        norm = normalize_arabic(txt)
        if vnorm in norm:
            found.append((v, i, entry, norm))
len(found)
